title: Smoky Italian Pistachio Tiramisu
description: Rich, creamy, and smoky, this Italian dessert is elevated with the fresh flavor of unsalted pistachios. Served in a rustic terracotta dish, it's a true Italian classic.

ingredients.name: strong espresso
ingredients.notes: use high-quality coffee beans for best results

ingredients.name: granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: use high-quality sugar for best flavor

ingredients.name: masala chilies
ingredients.notes: dried and smoked for added smokiness

instructions: Dip each ladyfinger into the espresso for 3-5 seconds on each side, until they are soft and pliable.
In a large mixing bowl, whip the mascarpone cheese and whipping cream until stiff peaks form.
Add the granulated sugar and vanilla extract to the mascarpone mixture and whip until combined.
Fold in the egg yolks and mascarpone mixture until no white streaks remain.
Add the salt, unsalted butter, and masala chilies to the mixture and mix until combined.
Assemble the tiramisu by placing a layer of ladyfingers in the bottom of a terracotta dish, followed by a layer of the mascarpone mixture.
Repeat the layers two more times, ending with a layer of mascarpone on top.
Dust the top of the tiramisu with unsweetened cocoa powder.
Cook in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is set and the edges are lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature before serving.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving.
Serve chilled, garnished with fresh pistachios if desired.
